sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 10.37 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.10)

statement.latex: Suppose that$G$is a finitely generated metabelian group all of whose integral homology groups are finitely generated. Is it true that$G$is a group of finite rank? The answer is affirmative if$G$splits over the derived subgroup (J.\,R.\,J.\,Groves, Quart.~J.~Math., { 33}, no.\,132 (1982), 405--420). { G.\,A.\,Noskov} Yes, it is (D.\,H.\,Kochloukova, Groups St. Andrews 2001 in Oxford, Vol. II, Cambr...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
